message:
wa-upg.12.7: add backpressure + scheduler performance benchmarks

Criterion benchmark suite covering backpressure tier classification,
hysteresis evaluation, queue depth sampling, pane pause management,
capture scheduler select_panes, budget checks, record_capture,
snapshot generation, and hot-reload. All results well within budgets
(classify ~5ns/<100ns, evaluate ~15ns/<200ns, select_panes ~51ns/<5µs,
snapshot ~3ns/<200ns).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-upg.12.4","title":"Surface backpressure warnings in status/doctor/triage","description":"# Task: Surface backpressure warnings

## Goal
Make backpressure obvious to users.

## Requirements
- `wa status` / `wa doctor` show:
  - queue depths
  - ingest lag
  - index lag
  - warnings when thresholds exceeded
- `wa triage` prioritizes backpressure incidents appropriately.

## Testing
- Output tests for stable formatting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can see when wa is behind and what to do next.

 {"id":"wa-upg.12.5","title":"Tests: slow consumer/backpressure integration tests","description":"# Task: Tests for backpressure handling

## Goal
Validate backpressure handling in deterministic integration tests.

## Requirements
- Simulate slow storage writer and assert:
  - adaptive polling triggers
  - queues remain bounded
  - GAP emitted when necessary

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration tests cover the failure modes that cause deadlocks/OOM.

 {"id":"wa-upg.12.6","title":"E2E: backpressure stress scenario (graceful degradation + artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E backpressure stress scenario

## Goal
Prove backpressure behavior in an end-to-end run.

## Scenario
- Generate high-output workload (prefer deterministic fixture playback).
- Verify:
  - watcher remains responsive
  - health surfaces show lag
  - GAP emitted when continuity cannot be guaranteed

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use quiescence.
- Artifacts:
  - health snapshots over time
  - logs
  - DB slice of gaps/events

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates bounded behavior and debuggability.

 {"id":"wa-upg.12.8","title":"Docs: backpressure behavior (signals, GAPs, operator troubleshooting)","description":"# Task: Document backpressure + graceful degradation

## Goal
Document how wa behaves under load so users/operators can:
- understand why capture latency increases
- know how to interpret GAP/backpressure warnings
- safely tune thresholds

## Content outline
- What backpressure means in wa:
  - bounded queues
  - lag metrics
- What the system does under backpressure:
  - adaptive slow-down
  - batching
  - GAP insertion (when continuity can't be guaranteed)
- How to troubleshoot:
  - which commands to run (`wa status`, `wa doctor`, `wa triage`)
  - what metrics/logs to inspect
  - safe mitigations (reduce observed panes, adjust polling, raise budgets)
- How to tune (config):
  - thresholds and their tradeoffs

## Acceptance Criteria
- A future operator can debug "why is wa behind?" without consulting PLAN.md.
- Docs are explicit about failure modes and how the system degrades safely.

 {"id":"wa-upg.13","title":"[EPIC] Crash-only operation + automatic crash bundles (diagnosable crashes)","description":"# [EPIC] Crash-only + automatic crash bundles

## Mission
Treat crashes as first-class events with artifacts:
- if the watcher crashes, we get a crash bundle automatically
- recovery paths are clear and safe
- crashes are diagnosable without guesswork

## Why This Matters
Crashes are inevitable in early development. The problem is not the crash; it's the mystery.
A crash-only posture means:
- crash → bundle → triage → reproduce

## Scope
- Panic/crash hook writes a bounded, redacted crash report.
- Crash bundle includes:
  - stack trace (if available)
  - last N events
  - health snapshot (queues/lag)
  - correlation IDs
  - pointer to diagnostic bundle tooling
- CLI surfaces:
  - `wa crashes` / `wa crash latest` (optional)
  - `wa doctor` shows recent crash

## Success Criteria
- When a crash occurs, users immediately see:
  - that it crashed
  - where the crash bundle is
  - what to do next

## Testing
- Integration tests that force a panic and assert crash bundle is created and redacted.
- E2E: crash the watcher intentionally and verify recovery + artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Crash bundle generation is deterministic, redacted, and bounded.
- Crashes appear in triage/status/doctor with actionable guidance.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:53:27.203544400Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:04:48.929643360Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-diagnostics","area-reliability","area-tests","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.13","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.13.1","title":"Design: crash bundle contents + privacy budget","description":"# Task: Design crash bundle

## Goal
Specify what a crash bundle contains and how it stays safe-to-share.

## Requirements
- Contents:
  - crash metadata (time, version, workspace)
  - stack trace/backtrace when available
  - last N events/detections and last M log lines (bounded)
  - health snapshot (queues/lag)
- Privacy budget:
  - max bytes per file
  - max total bytes
  - redaction rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- Design is implementable and aligned with incident bundle/reproduce flows.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:53:39.050755747Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T06:00:24.089778178Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T06:00:24.089649358Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-reliability","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.13.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
